text.title: spatial-interaction-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the spatial-interaction-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every critical interaction MUST declare one primary modality AND one fallback modality chosen from {hand-direct, hand-raycast, controller, gaze+dwell, voice, gesture}. Fallback MUST be independent of primary.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Meta Quest Interaction Documentation

rule.rationale: Single-modality interactions exclude users with motor differences and break on hardware degradation.

rule.statement: Each interaction MUST be modelled as a state machine with at least {idle, hover, active, cancel, error} states. Missing cancel/error states are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Missing cancel/error states cause stuck UI when input is lost mid-interaction.

rule.statement: Custom gestures MUST be drawn from a closed vocabulary documented in the spec. Adding a novel gesture requires explicit listing + discoverability hint.
rule.rationale: Novel undocumented gestures fail learnability tests; users default to platform-standard gestures and get stuck.

rule.statement: Same interaction MUST work on every declared platform with the modality available on that platform. Spec MUST document per-platform divergence explicitly.
rule.rationale: Hidden per-platform divergence creates surprise bugs and review failures.

rule.statement: Voice MUST be treated as a first-class modality, not a marketing-only feature. Voice intents MUST appear in the spec with same depth as hand interactions.
rule.rationale: Voice is the most reliable accessibility fallback; demoting it to optional excludes motor-impaired users.
